heading: Interlocutory appeals by the United States
label: 950d Interlocutory appeals by the United States
notes: Prior ProvisionsA prior section 950d, added , , , related to appeal by the United States, prior to the general amendment of this chapter by .Pub. L. 109–366, § 3(a)(1)120 Stat. 2620Pub. L. 111–84Oct. 17, 2006
amendments: Prior ProvisionsA prior section 950d, added , , , related to appeal by the United States, prior to the general amendment of this chapter by .Pub. L. 109–366, § 3(a)(1)120 Stat. 2620Pub. L. 111–84Oct. 17, 2006

children.text: that terminates proceedings of the military commission with respect to a charge or specification;
that excludes evidence that is substantial proof of a fact material in the proceeding;
that relates to a matter under subsection (c) or (d) of ; orsection 949d of this title

children.label: A
children.text: authorizes the disclosure of such information;

children.label: B
children.text: imposes sanctions for nondisclosure of such information; or

children.label: C
children.text: refuses a protective order sought by the United States to prevent the disclosure of such information.

children.heading: .—Limitation
children.label: b .—Limitation
children.text: The United States may not appeal under subsection (a) an order or ruling that is, or amounts to, a finding of not guilty by the military commission with respect to a charge or specification.

children.heading: .—Scope of Appeal Right With Respect to Classified Information
children.label: c .—Scope of Appeal Right With Respect to Classified Information
children.text: The United States has the right to appeal under paragraph (4) of subsection (a) whenever the military judge enters an order or ruling that would require the disclosure of classified information, without regard to whether the order or ruling appealed from was entered under this chapter, another provision of law, a rule, or otherwise. Any such appeal may embrace any preceding order, ruling, or reasoning constituting the basis of the order or ruling that would authorize such disclosure.

children.heading: Timing and Action on Interlocutory Appeals Relating to Classified Information.—
children.label: d Timing and Action on Interlocutory Appeals Relating to Classified Information.—

children.heading: .—Appeal to be expedited
children.label: 1 .—Appeal to be expedited
children.text: An appeal taken pursuant to paragraph (4) of subsection (a) shall be expedited by the United States Court of Military Commission Review.

children.heading: .—Appeals before trial
children.label: 2 .—Appeals before trial
children.text: If such an appeal is taken before trial, the appeal shall be taken within 10 days after the order or ruling from which the appeal is made and the trial shall not commence until the appeal is decided.

children.label: A
children.text: shall hear argument on such appeal within 4 days of the adjournment of the trial (excluding weekends and holidays);

children.label: B
children.text: may dispense with written briefs other than the supporting materials previously submitted to the military judge;

children.label: C
children.text: shall render its decision within four days of argument on appeal (excluding weekends and holidays); and

children.label: D
children.text: may dispense with the issuance of a written opinion in rendering its decision.

children.heading: .—Notice and Timing of Other Appeals
children.label: e .—Notice and Timing of Other Appeals
children.text: The United States shall take an appeal of an order or ruling under subsection (a), other than an appeal under paragraph (4) of that subsection, by filing a notice of appeal with the military judge within 5 days after the date of the order or ruling.

children.heading: .—Method of Appeal
children.label: f .—Method of Appeal
children.text: An appeal under this section shall be forwarded, by means specified in regulations prescribed by the Secretary of Defense, directly to the United States Court of Military Commission Review.

children.heading: .—Appeals Court To Act Only With Respect to Matter of Law
children.label: g .—Appeals Court To Act Only With Respect to Matter of Law
children.text: In ruling on an appeal under paragraph (1), (2), or (3) of subsection (a), the appeals court may act only with respect to matters of law.

children.heading: .—Subsequent Appeal Rights of Accused Not Affected
children.label: h .—Subsequent Appeal Rights of Accused Not Affected
children.text: An appeal under paragraph (4) of subsection (a), and a decision on such appeal, shall not affect the right of the accused, in a subsequent appeal from a judgment of conviction, to claim as error reversal by the military judge on remand of a ruling appealed from during trial.
